skill.name: adapt
skill.description: Adapt product interfaces and design patterns for mobile, tablet, desktop, print, email, and other usage contexts.
skill.summary: Guides responsive, platform-aware interface adaptation across devices and contexts.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed and found to be false positives in Markdown design guidance. Inline code spans describe CSS features, and weak cryptography hits are substring matches inside design terms. No evidence found of scripts, command execution, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at CSS guidance lines. These are Markdown inline code spans for CSS terms such as clamp(), display: none, srcset, and picture, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown documentation, not inside a script or executable file. No command interpreter, command substitution, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Terms Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms across the document. The flagged lines contain design guidance words such as design, desktop, device, and description, not cryptographic code or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is plain instructional Markdown with no crypto library calls or algorithm configuration. The matches are semantic false positives from ordinary design vocabulary.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Design Guidance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at tablet and CSS guidance lines. The reviewed content describes a hybrid tablet approach and CSS clamp(), with no operating system commands or host inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are prose or Markdown inline CSS examples. No shell command, filesystem access, environment query, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Designs often break when moved to new devices or contexts. This skill guides responsive, platform-aware adaptations that preserve core functionality.
actual_capabilities: Assesses source and target contexts before adapting a design.
Recommends layout strategies for mobile, tablet, desktop, print, and email.
Guides interaction changes for touch, pointer, keyboard, and hybrid input.
Suggests content prioritization, progressive disclosure, and navigation changes.
Provides testing checks for devices, orientations, browsers, operating systems, and connections.
limitations: It provides design guidance, not automated UI implementation.
It does not inspect live devices or run browser tests by itself.
It cannot guarantee accessibility compliance without project-specific review.
It does not replace user research for platform expectations.

use_cases.title: Convert Desktop Features to Mobile
use_cases.description: Plan how large layouts, complex navigation, and hover interactions should change for touch screens.

use_cases.title: Prepare Web Content for Email
use_cases.description: Adapt web layouts into narrow, single-column email experiences with clear calls to action.

use_cases.title: Review Responsive Implementation Plans
use_cases.description: Check breakpoints, layout reflow, touch targets, and testing coverage before engineering work begins.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one interface element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this component for mobile. Focus on layout, touch targets, content priority, and navigation changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Cross-Device Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an adaptation plan for this feature across mobile, tablet, and desktop. Include layout, input, content, and testing notes.

prompt_templates.title: Transform Web to Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this web experience into an email-friendly version. Address width, layout, CSS limits, calls to action, and fallback content.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Adaptation Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this adaptation strategy for missing constraints, risky assumptions, platform mismatches, and verification gaps.

output_examples.input: A desktop dashboard needs a mobile version.
output: Primary metrics move to the top of a single-column layout.
Secondary panels collapse into tabs or accordions.
Filters become a bottom sheet with clear apply and reset actions.
Tables need summary cards or horizontal alternatives for small screens.

output_examples.input: A web article must also work as a printed handout.
output: Remove navigation, hover-only content, and interactive controls.
Add page numbers, print margins, and visible URLs for links.
Convert charts and images to high-contrast print-friendly versions.

output_examples.input: A mobile onboarding flow needs a desktop version.
output: Use wider space for side-by-side guidance and preview content.
Expose supporting details without overwhelming the main task.
Add keyboard-friendly controls and visible progress indicators.

best_practices: Start with user context before choosing breakpoints or layout changes.
Preserve core functionality across every supported device and platform.
Test on real devices, orientations, browsers, and input methods.
anti_patterns: Do not hide essential features only because the screen is smaller.
Do not rely on hover behavior for touch-first contexts.
Do not use generic breakpoints without checking where content breaks.

faq.question: What does this skill help adapt?
faq.answer: It helps adapt interfaces, components, navigation, content, and interaction patterns across devices and contexts.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready responsive code?
faq.answer: No. It gives design and implementation guidance that a designer or engineer can apply.

faq.question: Does it cover mobile and desktop both ways?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers desktop-to-mobile, mobile-to-desktop, tablet, print, and email adaptation.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: It includes related guidance such as touch target size and input methods, but it is not a full accessibility audit.

faq.question: How should I provide context?
faq.answer: Share the current design, original context, target context, key workflows, and constraints.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown design guidance with no confirmed dangerous behavior.
